concepts.name: Print Concepts
concepts.description: Awareness that print carries meaning and follows conventional directionality (left-to-right, top-to-bottom) and word boundaries.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Phonemic Awareness
concepts.description: The ability to identify and manipulate individual sounds (phonemes) in spoken words, independent of print.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Alphabet Recognition
concepts.description: Recognizing and naming the twenty-six uppercase and lowercase letters of the English alphabet.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Rhyming
concepts.description: Recognizing and producing words that share ending sounds, an early phonological awareness skill.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Blending and Segmenting
concepts.description: Combining individual sounds into whole words (blending) and breaking words into their component sounds (segmenting).
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Letter-Sound Correspondence
concepts.description: The systematic mapping between written letters (graphemes) and their corresponding spoken sounds (phonemes).
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Consonant Sounds
concepts.description: The single-letter consonant phonemes of English and their standard spellings.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Short Vowel Sounds
concepts.description: The five short vowel phonemes (a, e, i, o, u) in closed one-syllable words such as "cat" and "pen".
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Consonant Blends
concepts.description: Two or three adjacent consonants whose individual sounds are each pronounced, such as "bl", "str", "sk".
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Consonant and Vowel Digraphs
concepts.description: Two-letter combinations representing a single new sound, such as "sh", "ch", "th", and "ai".
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Long Vowels and Silent E
concepts.description: The long vowel sounds and the silent-e pattern that lengthens a preceding vowel, as in "cake" versus "cak".
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: High-Frequency Sight Words
concepts.description: Common words that are recognized instantly by sight, including many with irregular spelling-sound correspondences.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Syllable Types
concepts.description: The six standard English syllable patterns (closed, open, silent-e, vowel team, r-controlled, consonant-le) used to decode multisyllabic words.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Decoding Fluency
concepts.description: The ability to read connected text accurately, automatically, and with appropriate expression, integrating all foundational phonics skills.
references: National Reading Panel (2000) Teaching Children to Read
Common Core State Standards — Foundational Skills (K-5)

concepts.name: Overview of Speech Sounds
concepts.description: An introduction to how spoken English sounds are produced, classified, and studied as physical events distinct from spelling.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Organs of Articulation
concepts.description: The vocal tract structures (lips, teeth, tongue, palate, vocal folds) and how their positioning shapes speech sounds.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Consonant Phoneme Classification
concepts.description: Classifying English consonant phonemes by place of articulation, manner of articulation, and voicing.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Vowel Phoneme Classification
concepts.description: Classifying English vowel phonemes by tongue height, tongue backness, and lip rounding, including monophthongs and diphthongs.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: IPA Basics for English
concepts.description: The International Phonetic Alphabet symbols used to represent the standard phonemes of English.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Minimal Pairs
concepts.description: Pairs of words that differ by exactly one phoneme, used to teach and test perception of sound contrasts.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Word Stress
concepts.description: The relative prominence given to one syllable within a multisyllabic word, which can distinguish meaning and word class.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Sentence Stress
concepts.description: The pattern of emphasized content words versus reduced function words across a spoken sentence.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Intonation Patterns
concepts.description: Rising and falling pitch patterns across an utterance that signal meaning distinctions such as statements, questions, and emphasis.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Connected Speech
concepts.description: Features of natural spoken English including elision, assimilation, and linking that alter how words sound in sequence.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Rhythm and Timing
concepts.description: The stress-timed rhythm of English, in which stressed syllables recur at roughly regular intervals regardless of unstressed syllables between them.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Accents and Dialects
concepts.description: Systematic regional and social variation in pronunciation across varieties of English.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Phonetic Transcription
concepts.description: Transcribing spoken English words and utterances using IPA symbols.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Prosody
concepts.description: The combined system of stress, rhythm, and intonation that conveys meaning, attitude, and emotion beyond individual word sounds.
references: Roach, P. English Phonetics and Phonology
Cambridge English Pronunciation in Use

concepts.name: Word Recognition
concepts.description: Recognizing printed words as meaningful units linked to spoken vocabulary already known.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Context Clues
concepts.description: Using surrounding words and sentences to infer the meaning of an unfamiliar word.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Synonyms and Antonyms
concepts.description: Words with the same or similar meaning (synonyms) and words with opposite meaning (antonyms).
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Word Families
concepts.description: Groups of words that share a common base word or root and related meaning.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Compound Words
concepts.description: Words formed by combining two or more independent words into a single new word.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Prefixes
concepts.description: Word parts added to the beginning of a base word or root that change its meaning.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Suffixes
concepts.description: Word parts added to the end of a base word or root that change its meaning or grammatical category.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Greek and Latin Roots
concepts.description: Common Greek and Latin word roots underlying a large portion of English academic and scientific vocabulary.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Word Formation Processes
concepts.description: The systematic processes — derivation, compounding, conversion, blending, and clipping — by which English creates new words.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Homonyms and Homophones
concepts.description: Words that are spelled alike but differ in meaning (homonyms), and words that sound alike but differ in spelling and meaning (homophones).
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Multiple-Meaning Words
concepts.description: Single words with several distinct but related meanings (polysemy), disambiguated by context.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Connotation and Denotation
concepts.description: A word's literal dictionary meaning (denotation) versus its emotional or cultural association (connotation).
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Collocations
concepts.description: Combinations of words that habitually co-occur in natural English, such as "make a decision" rather than "do a decision".
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Idioms
concepts.description: Fixed expressions whose overall meaning cannot be derived from the literal meaning of their individual words.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Phrasal Verbs
concepts.description: Verb-plus-particle combinations, such as "give up" or "look after", that function as a single semantic unit.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Register and Formality
concepts.description: Choosing vocabulary appropriate to a communicative context, ranging from informal/colloquial to formal/academic register.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Academic Vocabulary
concepts.description: General, discipline-neutral vocabulary that recurs frequently across academic texts (the Academic Word List tradition).
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Etymology
concepts.description: The historical origin and semantic change of words over time, including borrowing between languages.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Thesaurus and Dictionary Skills
concepts.description: Efficient and accurate use of dictionaries and thesauruses, including interpreting entries, parts of speech, and usage labels.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Semantic Fields
concepts.description: Groups of words related in meaning that structure a shared conceptual domain, such as vocabulary for emotions or weather.
references: Nation, I.S.P. Learning Vocabulary in Another Language
Oxford Word Skills

concepts.name: Overview of Word Classes
concepts.description: An introduction to the eight traditional parts of speech as functional categories of English words.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Nouns
concepts.description: Words naming people, places, things, or ideas, including common, proper, countable, uncountable, and collective nouns.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Pronouns
concepts.description: Words that substitute for nouns, including personal, possessive, reflexive, relative, and indefinite pronouns.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Verbs
concepts.description: Words expressing action or state of being, including main verbs and auxiliary (helping) verbs.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Adjectives
concepts.description: Words that modify nouns by describing quality, quantity, or type.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Adverbs
concepts.description: Words that modify verbs, adjectives, or other adverbs, typically expressing manner, time, place, or degree.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Prepositions
concepts.description: Words that show the relationship of a noun or pronoun to another word in the sentence, typically expressing location, time, or direction.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Conjunctions
concepts.description: Words that connect words, phrases, or clauses, including coordinating, subordinating, and correlative conjunctions.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Interjections
concepts.description: Words or phrases expressing sudden emotion, typically standing independently of the surrounding sentence structure.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Articles and Determiners
concepts.description: Words such as "a", "an", "the", and other determiners that specify or quantify the noun they precede.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Subject and Predicate
concepts.description: The two fundamental structural parts of an English sentence: the subject (who or what) and the predicate (what is said about it).
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Basic Word Order
concepts.description: The canonical Subject-Verb-Object word order of English declarative sentences.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Sentence Types by Function
concepts.description: The four functional sentence types: declarative, interrogative, imperative, and exclamatory.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Question Formation
concepts.description: Forming yes/no questions and wh-questions through subject-auxiliary inversion and question words.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Negation
concepts.description: Forming negative sentences using "not", auxiliary verbs, and negative words such as "never" and "no".
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Phrases
concepts.description: Groups of related words functioning as a single grammatical unit without a subject-verb pair: noun, verb, prepositional, adjectival, and adverbial phrases.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Independent and Dependent Clauses
concepts.description: Word groups containing a subject and a verb; independent clauses can stand alone while dependent clauses cannot.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Simple Sentences
concepts.description: Sentences consisting of exactly one independent clause.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Compound Sentences
concepts.description: Sentences formed by joining two or more independent clauses with a coordinating conjunction, semicolon, or conjunctive adverb.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Complex Sentences
concepts.description: Sentences formed by joining an independent clause with one or more dependent clauses.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Sentence Combining
concepts.description: Deliberately varying sentence length and structure by combining simple sentences into compound and complex forms for fluency and style.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Present Tenses
concepts.description: The simple, continuous, perfect, and perfect continuous present tense forms and their uses.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Past Tenses
concepts.description: The simple, continuous, perfect, and perfect continuous past tense forms and their uses.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Future Tenses
concepts.description: The various ways English expresses future time, including "will", "going to", and present forms used for scheduled events.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Tense Consistency
concepts.description: Maintaining logically consistent verb tense across a sentence or passage, avoiding unmotivated tense shifts.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Modal Verbs
concepts.description: Auxiliary verbs such as can, could, may, might, must, shall, should, will, and would that express ability, possibility, permission, or obligation.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Conditional Sentences
concepts.description: The zero, first, second, and third conditional structures expressing real, possible, hypothetical, and counterfactual conditions.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Active and Passive Voice
concepts.description: Sentence structures in which the subject performs the action (active) or receives the action (passive).
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Direct and Indirect Speech
concepts.description: Reporting another person's exact words (direct speech) versus paraphrasing them with appropriate tense and pronoun shifts (indirect/reported speech).
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Gerunds and Infinitives
concepts.description: The -ing verb form used as a noun (gerund) and the "to + base verb" form (infinitive), and the verbs that govern each.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Participles and Participial Phrases
concepts.description: Present and past participle forms used as adjectives or within participial phrases modifying a noun.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Subject-Verb Agreement
concepts.description: The grammatical rule that a verb must agree in number and person with its subject.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Pronoun-Antecedent Agreement
concepts.description: The rule that a pronoun must agree in number and gender with the noun it refers back to.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Comparatives and Superlatives
concepts.description: Adjective and adverb forms used to compare two items (comparative) or three or more items (superlative).
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Parallel Structure
concepts.description: Using matching grammatical forms for items joined in a series or comparison, avoiding mismatched structures.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Capitalization Rules
concepts.description: Conventions governing when letters must be capitalized: sentence beginnings, proper nouns, titles, and the pronoun "I".
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: End Punctuation
concepts.description: The period, question mark, and exclamation point used to close a sentence according to its function.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Comma Usage
concepts.description: Rules governing commas in lists, compound sentences, introductory elements, and nonessential information.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Apostrophes
concepts.description: Using apostrophes to show possession and to mark omitted letters in contractions.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Quotation Marks
concepts.description: Punctuation conventions for marking direct quotations, titles of short works, and dialogue.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Colons, Semicolons, and Dashes
concepts.description: Advanced punctuation marks used to join related clauses, introduce lists or explanations, and add emphasis.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Sentence Fragments
concepts.description: Incomplete sentences lacking a subject, a verb, or a complete thought, and strategies for correcting them.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: Run-On Sentences and Comma Splices
concepts.description: Errors where two independent clauses are incorrectly joined without proper punctuation or a conjunction, and how to correct them.
references: Quirk, Greenbaum, Leech & Svartvik A Comprehensive Grammar of the English Language
Swan, M. Practical English Usage

concepts.name: From Print to Meaning
concepts.description: The bridging skill of connecting accurately decoded print to comprehended meaning, uniting decoding and vocabulary knowledge.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Reading Fluency
concepts.description: Reading connected text accurately, at an appropriate rate, and with expression that reflects meaning.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Literal Comprehension
concepts.description: Understanding information that is explicitly stated in a text.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Main Idea and Supporting Details
concepts.description: Identifying a text's central idea and the specific details that develop or support it.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Inference in Reading
concepts.description: Drawing logical conclusions from textual evidence that is implied but not directly stated.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Summarizing
concepts.description: Condensing a text's essential content into a concise restatement in one's own words.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Predicting and Confirming
concepts.description: Forming reasonable predictions about upcoming text content and revising them as new information is read.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Text Structure
concepts.description: Recognizing organizational patterns in text, including narrative sequence, cause-effect, compare-contrast, problem-solution, and description.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Genre Recognition
concepts.description: Identifying a text's genre (fiction, nonfiction, poetry, drama, and their subtypes) and the conventions associated with it.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Author's Purpose and Tone
concepts.description: Identifying why an author wrote a text (to inform, persuade, entertain) and the attitude conveyed through word choice.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Comparing and Contrasting Texts
concepts.description: Analyzing similarities and differences between two or more texts on a shared topic, theme, or form.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Skimming and Scanning
concepts.description: Rapidly surveying a text for its general idea (skimming) or locating specific information (scanning).
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Close Reading
concepts.description: Careful, deliberate analysis of a short passage's precise language, structure, and meaning.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Critical Reading
concepts.description: Evaluating a text's credibility, evidence, assumptions, and bias rather than accepting its claims at face value.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Evaluating Sources
concepts.description: Assessing the reliability, authority, currency, and relevance of a written source.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Reading Across Genres
concepts.description: Synthesizing understanding across multiple texts and genres on a related topic or theme.
references: Common Core State Standards — Reading
British Council LearnEnglish Reading Skills

concepts.name: Handwriting and Letter Formation
concepts.description: Forming legible printed and cursive letters with correct stroke direction, size, and spacing.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Spelling Strategies
concepts.description: Applying phonics patterns, morphology, and visual memory to spell words accurately.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Sentence Writing
concepts.description: Composing grammatically complete, meaningful sentences.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Paragraph Structure
concepts.description: Organizing a group of related sentences around a single controlling idea.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Topic Sentences
concepts.description: Writing a sentence that states the main idea a paragraph will develop.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Supporting Details
concepts.description: Selecting and organizing specific facts, examples, and explanations that develop a topic sentence.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Transitions and Cohesion
concepts.description: Using transitional words and phrases to link ideas smoothly within and between paragraphs.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Narrative Writing
concepts.description: Writing that recounts a sequence of real or imagined events, developed through characters, setting, and plot.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Descriptive Writing
concepts.description: Writing that creates a vivid impression of a person, place, object, or experience through sensory detail.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Expository Writing
concepts.description: Writing that explains, informs, or clarifies a topic using facts, definitions, and examples.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Persuasive Writing Basics
concepts.description: Writing that states a position and supports it with reasons intended to convince a reader.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: The Writing Process
concepts.description: The recursive stages of planning, drafting, revising, editing, and publishing that structure effective composition.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Outlining and Planning
concepts.description: Organizing ideas and evidence into a logical structure before drafting.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Drafting
concepts.description: Producing an initial full version of a piece of writing focused on content over polish.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Revising for Content
concepts.description: Reworking a draft's ideas, organization, and evidence for clarity and effectiveness.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Editing and Proofreading
concepts.description: Correcting grammar, spelling, punctuation, and mechanical errors in a near-final draft.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Essay Structure
concepts.description: The conventional multi-paragraph essay form: introduction, body paragraphs, and conclusion.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Thesis Statements
concepts.description: A single sentence that states an essay's central argument or purpose and previews its development.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Citations and Referencing
concepts.description: Formally acknowledging sources used in writing, following a consistent citation style.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Creative Writing Forms
concepts.description: Exploring imaginative writing forms beyond standard prose, including flash fiction, dialogue, and free verse.
references: Common Core State Standards — Writing
Oxford Guide to Effective Writing and Speaking

concepts.name: Active Listening
concepts.description: Attending fully to a speaker, including verbal and nonverbal signals of engagement and understanding.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Listening for Gist
concepts.description: Understanding the overall topic or general meaning of spoken language without processing every word.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Listening for Detail
concepts.description: Extracting specific factual information from spoken language.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Distinguishing Sounds in Speech
concepts.description: Perceiving fine phonetic contrasts between similar-sounding words in connected speech.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Following Spoken Instructions
concepts.description: Understanding and correctly executing a sequence of spoken directions.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Note-Taking While Listening
concepts.description: Recording key information from spoken language efficiently while continuing to listen.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Listening Comprehension Strategies
concepts.description: Metacognitive strategies such as predicting, inferring, and monitoring understanding while listening.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Critical Listening
concepts.description: Evaluating a speaker's argument, evidence, and possible bias while listening.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Listening Scales
Cambridge English Listening Skills

concepts.name: Oral Fluency
concepts.description: Speaking at a natural pace with appropriate pausing and minimal hesitation.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Pronunciation in Conversation
concepts.description: Applying accurate pronunciation, stress, and intonation in spontaneous spoken interaction.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Conversation Skills
concepts.description: Managing turn-taking, initiating and closing conversations, and maintaining topical coherence in dialogue.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Asking and Answering Questions
concepts.description: Formulating clear spoken questions and providing relevant, complete spoken answers.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Oral Storytelling
concepts.description: Recounting a narrative aloud with clear sequencing, expression, and audience engagement.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Discussion Skills
concepts.description: Contributing productively to group discussion, including building on others' ideas and respectfully disagreeing.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Public Speaking Basics
concepts.description: Speaking clearly and confidently to an audience, including basic techniques for managing nervousness.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Presentation Skills
concepts.description: Organizing and delivering a structured spoken presentation supported by visual or other aids.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Debate Skills
concepts.description: Constructing and orally defending an argument, and rebutting an opposing position, within a structured format.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Non-Verbal Communication
concepts.description: Body language, eye contact, gesture, and facial expression as components of spoken communication.
references: CEFR Companion Volume — Spoken Production/Interaction Scales
Cambridge English Speaking Skills

concepts.name: Audience and Purpose
concepts.description: Analyzing the intended reader and communicative goal to guide choices in a piece of writing.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Claim, Evidence, and Reasoning
concepts.description: The structure of a sound argument: a claim, the evidence supporting it, and the reasoning connecting evidence to claim.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Argumentation Basics
concepts.description: Constructing a logically structured argument that states and defends a position.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Counterargument and Rebuttal
concepts.description: Anticipating opposing viewpoints and responding to them to strengthen an argument's persuasiveness.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Logical Fallacies
concepts.description: Common errors in reasoning that undermine an argument's validity, such as ad hominem, straw man, and false dilemma.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Rhetorical Appeals: Ethos, Pathos, Logos
concepts.description: The three classical modes of persuasion: appeals to credibility (ethos), emotion (pathos), and logic (logos).
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Rhetorical Devices
concepts.description: Deliberate stylistic and structural techniques — such as repetition, parallelism, and antithesis — used to enhance persuasive or expressive effect.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Figurative Language in Composition
concepts.description: Using metaphor, imagery, and other non-literal language purposefully in expository and persuasive writing.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Style, Voice, and Tone
concepts.description: The distinctive choices of diction, sentence structure, and attitude that constitute a writer's individual style.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Advanced Persuasive Techniques
concepts.description: Combining rhetorical appeals with counterargument and evidence to construct sophisticated persuasive writing.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Rhetorical Analysis
concepts.description: Analyzing how a text's rhetorical choices achieve its persuasive or communicative effect on its audience.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Comparative Essay Writing
concepts.description: Writing an essay that systematically analyzes similarities and differences between two texts, ideas, or works.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Research Paper Writing
concepts.description: Producing an extended, evidence-based paper that synthesizes multiple sources around an original thesis.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Academic Writing Conventions
concepts.description: The formal conventions of academic prose, including objectivity, hedging, and disciplinary style.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Plagiarism and Citation Ethics
concepts.description: The ethical and academic-integrity principles governing proper attribution of others' ideas and words.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Editing for Style
concepts.description: Revising a draft specifically for concision, precision, and stylistic consistency, beyond correctness alone.
references: Aristotle Rhetoric (trans. Kennedy)
Corbett & Connors Classical Rhetoric for the Modern Student

concepts.name: Narrative Elements
concepts.description: The fundamental components of a story: plot, character, setting, and conflict.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Plot Structure
concepts.description: The conventional arc of a narrative: exposition, rising action, climax, falling action, and resolution.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Character Development
concepts.description: How authors reveal and evolve character through action, dialogue, and description, including protagonist, antagonist, and characterization technique.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Setting and Atmosphere
concepts.description: The time and place of a narrative and the mood it evokes for the reader.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Point of View
concepts.description: The narrative perspective from which a story is told: first person, second person, third person limited, and third person omniscient.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Theme and Message
concepts.description: The central underlying idea or insight about life that a literary work explores.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Overview of Literary Devices
concepts.description: An introduction to the figurative, structural, and sound-based techniques writers use to create literary effect.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Metaphor and Simile
concepts.description: Figurative comparisons that describe one thing directly as another (metaphor) or explicitly using "like"/"as" (simile).
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Symbolism
concepts.description: The use of an object, person, or event to represent a larger idea beyond its literal meaning.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Irony
concepts.description: Verbal, situational, and dramatic irony, in which meaning or outcome contrasts with expectation.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Foreshadowing and Suspense
concepts.description: Narrative techniques that hint at future events and generate reader anticipation or tension.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Imagery
concepts.description: Language that evokes sensory experience — sight, sound, touch, taste, and smell — in the reader.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Poetry Basics
concepts.description: The fundamental features distinguishing poetry from prose, including line, stanza, and condensed language.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Poetic Forms
concepts.description: Conventional poetic structures such as the sonnet, haiku, ballad, and free verse.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Meter and Rhyme
concepts.description: The patterns of stressed and unstressed syllables (meter) and end-sound repetition (rhyme scheme) in verse.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Drama Basics
concepts.description: The distinguishing features of drama as a genre performed for an audience: dialogue, stage directions, and acts/scenes.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Dramatic Structure
concepts.description: The organization of a play into acts and scenes and the conventions of dramatic tension and resolution.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Prose Fiction
concepts.description: Extended imaginative narrative in prose form, encompassing the short story and the novel.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Prose Nonfiction
concepts.description: Factual prose forms including the essay, memoir, biography, and journalism.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Short Story Study
concepts.description: Close analysis of the short story form, whose compression demands economy of plot, character, and theme.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Novel Study
concepts.description: Sustained analysis of a full-length novel's plot, character, theme, and structure across an extended narrative.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Overview of Literary Genres
concepts.description: A survey of the major literary genres — fiction, poetry, drama, and nonfiction — and their principal subtypes.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Survey of Literary Periods
concepts.description: A survey introduction to major literary-historical periods and movements in English-language literature.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Introduction to Literary Criticism
concepts.description: A survey of major critical approaches to interpreting literature, including historical, formalist, and reader-response criticism.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: Introduction to Comparative Literature
concepts.description: A survey introduction to studying literary works across languages, cultures, and traditions in relation to one another.
references: Abrams & Harpham A Glossary of Literary Terms
The Norton Anthology of English Literature

concepts.name: What Is Linguistics?
concepts.description: A survey introduction to linguistics as the scientific, descriptive study of human language.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Phonology
concepts.description: A survey of phonology as the study of the abstract sound system of a language, distinct from phonetics' study of physical sound.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Morphology
concepts.description: A survey of morphology as the study of word-internal structure and the morpheme as the smallest unit of meaning.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Syntactic Theory
concepts.description: A survey of syntax as the formal study of sentence structure, distinct from prescriptive school grammar.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Semantics
concepts.description: A survey of semantics as the study of linguistic meaning at the word and sentence level.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Pragmatics
concepts.description: A survey of pragmatics as the study of how context shapes meaning in language use.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Discourse Analysis
concepts.description: A survey of how meaning and coherence are constructed across stretches of connected text or talk beyond the single sentence.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Sociolinguistics
concepts.description: A survey of how language varies and functions in relation to social factors such as class, gender, and identity.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Historical Linguistics
concepts.description: A survey of how languages change over time in sound, structure, and meaning.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Language Families
concepts.description: A survey of the genealogical classification of the world's languages into related families, including the Indo-European family.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Language Acquisition
concepts.description: A survey of how children and learners acquire first and additional languages.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Psycholinguistics
concepts.description: A survey of the mental processes underlying language comprehension, production, and storage.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Applied Linguistics
concepts.description: A survey of practical applications of linguistic theory, including language teaching, testing, and policy.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Dialectology
concepts.description: The systematic study of regional and social dialects as a specialized branch of sociolinguistics.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Bilingualism and Multilingualism
concepts.description: A survey of how individuals and societies acquire, use, and are shaped by more than one language.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Translation Studies
concepts.description: A survey of the theory and practice of transferring meaning between languages.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Corpus Linguistics
concepts.description: A survey of using large, structured collections of authentic text to study language empirically.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Introduction to Computational Linguistics
concepts.description: A survey of computational and statistical approaches to processing and modeling human language.
references: Fromkin, Rodman & Hyams An Introduction to Language
Yule, G. The Study of Language

concepts.name: Advanced Academic Writing
concepts.description: Producing sophisticated academic prose for advanced coursework and research contexts, extending academic writing conventions.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Writing Research Methodology
concepts.description: Describing and justifying the methods used in an original research project in written form.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Technical Writing
concepts.description: Writing clear, precise, and audience-appropriate documentation of processes, products, or systems.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Business Writing
concepts.description: Writing clear, professional correspondence and documents for workplace contexts, including memos, reports, and emails.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Media Literacy
concepts.description: Critically analyzing and evaluating messages, sources, and techniques used across print, broadcast, and digital media.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Digital Communication
concepts.description: Conventions and register choices for email, messaging, and online discourse.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Advanced Discourse Markers
concepts.description: Sophisticated cohesive devices that signal logical relationships between ideas across extended spoken and written discourse.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Cross-Cultural Communication
concepts.description: Adapting communication style and expectations across different cultural and linguistic communities.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Professional Communication
concepts.description: Integrated written and spoken communication skills appropriate to professional and workplace settings.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Negotiation Language
concepts.description: Language strategies for reaching agreement, managing disagreement, and building consensus in spoken interaction.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Presentation Design
concepts.description: Designing visual and structural support materials that clarify and reinforce a spoken presentation.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)

concepts.name: Editing for Publication
concepts.description: Preparing a finished piece of writing to professional publication standards, including final proofreading and formatting.
references: Common Core Anchor Standards for College and Career Readiness
Purdue OWL (Online Writing Lab)
